seo.title: Government Customer Support AI Agent Checklist
seo.description: Production readiness checklist for customer support AI agents in government. Covers FedRAMP, FISMA, NIST 800-53.

content.intro: Deploying AI agents in government customer support environments carries extraordinary stakes: a single misconfiguration can expose classified data, violate citizens' constitutional rights, or trigger congressional oversight. Federal agencies must satisfy overlapping mandates from FedRAMP, FISMA, NIST 800-53, and the Executive Order on AI before any citizen-facing agent goes live. This checklist provides concrete, regulation-specific controls for a RAG-based customer support agent integrated with CRM and ticketing systems.

sections.heading: Data Classification & Sovereignty Controls
sections.description: Ensure the RAG pipeline, CRM integration, and knowledge base retrieval never expose classified, CUI, or PII data to unauthorized external systems or LLM providers.

items.label: Classify every data source feeding the RAG knowledge base
items.description: Tag each document and CRM record with its NIST SP 800-60 data classification (Public, CUI, Confidential) before ingesting into the vector store. Block any source classified above the agent's authorized processing level.

items.label: Enforce a hard block on classified data reaching external LLMs
items.description: Implement a pre-retrieval filter that inspects metadata tags and rejects any chunk marked CUI or above from being passed to the LLM API call. Log every rejection with timestamp, document ID, and user session ID.

items.label: Deploy the LLM inference layer within a FedRAMP High authorized boundary
items.description: Confirm your LLM provider holds a FedRAMP High P-ATO or operate a self-hosted model within your agency's own ATO boundary. Document the authorization package reference number in your system security plan.

items.label: Implement PII detection and redaction before CRM writes
items.description: Run every agent-generated ticket and order lookup result through a PII scanning layer (SSN, DOD ID, passport number patterns) before writing to the CRM. Use a NIST-validated tokenization scheme for any PII that must be retained.

items.label: Establish data residency controls for all vector store embeddings
items.description: Confirm embeddings are stored in US-region infrastructure only, with encryption at rest using FIPS 140-2 validated modules. Document the storage location in your FedRAMP System Security Plan (SSP).

items.label: Audit CRM integration credentials with least-privilege service accounts
items.description: Create a dedicated service account for the agent's CRM and ticketing tool calls with read/write scopes limited to citizen support records only. Rotate credentials every 90 days and log all API calls to a SIEM.

items.label: Define and test data retention and deletion procedures for conversation logs
items.description: Set conversation log retention to the minimum required by your agency's records schedule (typically 3 years for citizen interactions). Implement automated deletion workflows and test them quarterly.

sections.heading: Algorithmic Accountability & Explainability
sections.description: Satisfy the Executive Order on AI and agency policy requirements that all AI-assisted decisions affecting citizens be explainable, auditable, and subject to human appeal.

items.label: Complete an Algorithmic Impact Assessment (AIA) before go-live
items.description: Document the agent's decision logic, training data provenance, retrieval methodology, and potential disparate impacts on protected classes. Submit the AIA to your agency's AI governance board and obtain written approval before production deployment.

items.label: Implement citation-backed responses for every policy-related answer
items.description: Configure the RAG pipeline to append the source document title, section number, and publication date to every response that references a policy, regulation, or eligibility determination. Citizens must be able to verify the basis of any answer.

items.label: Build a human appeal pathway into every citizen-facing interaction
items.description: Every agent response that involves a determination (eligibility, status, denial) must include a clearly labeled option to request human review. Log appeal requests in the CRM with a mandatory SLA of 5 business days for human follow-up.

items.label: Maintain a model card and system card updated at each model version change
items.description: Document the LLM version, fine-tuning datasets, known limitations, and intended use scope in a model card stored in your agency's AI inventory. Update this card within 5 business days of any model upgrade or prompt change.

items.label: Log all tool-calling actions (ticket creation, order lookup) with full audit trails
items.description: Record every CRM write, ticket creation, and order lookup with the agent session ID, citizen identifier (tokenized), tool name, input parameters, and output. Store logs in a tamper-evident system for a minimum of 3 years.

items.label: Define and document escalation trigger logic with version control
items.description: Maintain a versioned configuration file listing all escalation triggers (e.g., keywords indicating distress, unresolved issues after 2 turns, requests for supervisor). Review and update triggers monthly with change log entries.

items.label: Conduct quarterly bias and disparate impact reviews on agent outputs
items.description: Sample 500 citizen interactions per quarter, stratified by demographic proxy signals available in CRM data, and test for statistically significant differences in response quality, escalation rates, or resolution times across groups.

sections.heading: Security Controls & FISMA/NIST 800-53 Compliance
sections.description: Implement the specific NIST 800-53 control families most critical to an AI agent with CRM integration and external tool-calling capabilities.

items.label: Obtain and maintain an Authority to Operate (ATO) covering the AI agent component
items.description: Ensure your agency's ATO explicitly names the AI agent, its LLM provider, vector store, and CRM integration as in-scope system components. Do not operate under a legacy ATO that predates the AI agent's addition to the architecture.

items.label: Implement AC-3 and AC-6 (Access Enforcement and Least Privilege) for all agent tool calls
items.description: Configure the agent's tool-calling layer so each tool (ticket creation, order lookup) requires explicit authorization per NIST AC-3. Deny all tool calls by default and whitelist only approved actions with documented justification.

items.label: Enable SI-10 (Information Input Validation) to block prompt injection attacks
items.description: Deploy an input validation layer that detects and blocks prompt injection patterns, jailbreak attempts, and attempts to extract system prompts. Test with OWASP LLM Top 10 attack vectors before go-live and after each model update.

items.label: Configure AU-2 and AU-12 (Audit Events and Audit Record Generation) for all agent actions
items.description: Define the specific auditable events for the AI agent: session start/end, retrieval queries, tool calls, escalations, PII filter triggers, and error states. Verify logs are forwarded to your agency SIEM within 60 seconds of event occurrence.

items.label: Implement IR-4 (Incident Handling) procedures specific to AI agent failure modes
items.description: Create an incident response runbook covering AI-specific scenarios: hallucinated policy guidance delivered to citizens, PII leak via CRM write, prompt injection success, and model unavailability. Assign named owners and test the runbook semi-annually.

items.label: Conduct penetration testing of the RAG pipeline and CRM integration annually
items.description: Commission a penetration test by a FedRAMP-approved third-party assessment organization (3PAO) that specifically targets the RAG retrieval boundary, CRM API endpoints, and tool-calling interface. Remediate all High findings within 30 days.

items.label: Apply CM-7 (Least Functionality) to disable unused LLM capabilities
items.description: Disable all LLM features not required for customer support (e.g., code execution, image generation, web browsing) at the API configuration level. Document disabled capabilities in the SSP and verify in quarterly configuration audits.

items.label: Implement SA-11 (Developer Security and Privacy Testing) for every prompt template change
items.description: Treat prompt template modifications as code changes subject to your agency's secure development lifecycle. Require peer review, automated safety testing, and approval from the system owner before deploying updated prompts to production.

sections.heading: Citizen Experience, Accessibility & Operational Verification
sections.description: Ensure the agent meets Section 508 accessibility requirements, maintains policy-accurate and appropriately toned responses, and has operational verification processes that catch failures before citizens are harmed.

items.label: Certify Section 508 conformance for all citizen-facing agent interfaces
items.description: Test the chat interface, escalation pathways, and any generated documents against WCAG 2.1 AA and Section 508 standards using both automated tools (axe, WAVE) and manual testing with assistive technology users. Document conformance in a VPAT.

items.label: Build a response accuracy verification pipeline with ground-truth policy documents
items.description: Maintain a curated test set of 200+ citizen questions with verified correct answers sourced from official policy documents. Run this test set against the agent weekly and alert if accuracy drops below 95% on any policy domain.

items.label: Implement real-time PII filter verification with synthetic test probes
items.description: Inject synthetic PII (fake SSNs, DOD IDs, addresses) into test queries daily in a staging environment and verify the PII filter blocks 100% of leakage to LLM and CRM outputs. Halt production deployment if filter pass rate drops below 100%.

items.label: Define and enforce tone and language standards aligned with plain language requirements
items.description: Create a tone rubric based on Plain Writing Act standards and your agency's style guide. Score a random sample of 50 agent responses weekly against the rubric. Flag responses scoring below threshold for prompt engineering review.

items.label: Configure and test all escalation triggers with documented citizen scenarios
items.description: Write test scripts for each escalation trigger scenario (e.g., citizen expresses frustration 3 times, issue unresolved after 3 turns, request involves legal action). Run these scripts in staging after every agent update and verify escalation fires correctly.

items.label: Establish a citizen feedback loop with mandatory review of negative ratings
items.description: Implement a post-interaction survey with a 1-5 rating and optional comment field. Route all 1-2 star ratings to a human reviewer within 24 hours. Aggregate feedback monthly to identify systematic accuracy or tone failures.

items.label: Publish a public-facing AI use notice on all citizen interaction channels
items.description: Display a clear, plain-language disclosure at the start of every agent interaction stating that the citizen is interacting with an AI system, what data is collected, and how to request human assistance. Legal must approve the disclosure language.

items.label: Conduct multilingual accuracy testing for all supported languages
items.description: If the agent supports languages other than English, run the accuracy test set in each supported language quarterly. Verify that policy citations, escalation triggers, and PII filters function correctly in all languages before enabling them for citizens.

notes.note: Any cloud service used in the agent architecture—including the LLM API, vector database, and CRM—must operate within a FedRAMP High authorized boundary given the critical sensitivity designation. Your agency's ISSO must verify each third-party component's authorization status in the FedRAMP Marketplace before integration, and any lapse in a provider's authorization requires immediate contingency action per your continuity of operations plan.

notes.regulation: Executive Order 14110 on Safe, Secure, and Trustworthy AI
notes.note: Section 10 of EO 14110 requires federal agencies to designate a Chief AI Officer, maintain an inventory of AI use cases, and conduct impact assessments for AI systems that interact with the public. Your government customer support agent must appear in your agency's AI use case inventory submitted to OMB, and the Algorithmic Impact Assessment must be completed and approved before the agent handles live citizen inquiries.

NIST's AI Risk Management Framework (AI RMF 1.0) is increasingly referenced in agency AI governance policies as the companion framework to 800-53 for AI-specific risks. Map your agent's controls to both the AI RMF's GOVERN, MAP, MEASURE, and MANAGE functions and the relevant 800-53 control families (AC, AU, SI, SA, IR). Document this dual mapping in your SSP to satisfy both FISMA auditors and agency AI governance reviewers.

Section 508 applies to all electronic and information technology procured or developed by federal agencies, explicitly including AI-powered interfaces. The agent's chat UI, any documents it generates (e.g., ticket confirmation PDFs), and the escalation pathway to human agents must all conform to WCAG 2.1 AA. Failure to achieve conformance exposes the agency to complaints filed with the Access Board and potential litigation under the Rehabilitation Act.

cta.heading: Automate Your Government Agent Compliance
cta.description: Use Vex to continuously monitor your government AI agent against FedRAMP, FISMA, and EO on AI requirements—catching compliance gaps before they become congressional investigations.
